name: disjoint-set

metadata.name: disjoint-set
metadata.summary: Disjoint set data structure implementation for Python

documentation:
# disjoint-set

![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/disjoint_set.svg)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/disjoint_set.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/disjoint-set/)
![Coveralls](https://img.shields.io/coveralls/github/mrapacz/disjoint-set/master.svg)
![PyPI - License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/disjoint_set.svg)

[DisjointSet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Disjoint-set_data_structure) (a.k.a. union–find data structure or merge–find set) implementation for Python.

## Prerequisites

The only requirement is using **Python 3.8+**. You can verify this by running:

```bash
$ python --version
Python 3.8.18
```

## Installation

```
pip install disjoint-set
```

You can verify you're running the latest package version by running:

```python
>>> import disjoint_set
>>> disjoint_set.__version__
'0.8.0'

```

## Usage

### Import & instantiate

```python
>>> from disjoint_set import DisjointSet
>>> DisjointSet()
DisjointSet({})

>>> DisjointSet({1: 1})
DisjointSet({1: 1})

>>> DisjointSet.from_iterable([1,2,3])
DisjointSet({1: 1, 2: 2, 3: 3})

```

### Perform find & union operations

```python
>>> ds = DisjointSet()
>>> ds.find(1)
1

>>> ds.union(1,2)
>>> ds.find(1)
2

>>> ds.find(2)
2

```

### Check if values belong to the same set

```python
>>> ds = DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2, 3: 3})
>>> ds.connected(1,2)
True

>>> ds.connected(1,3)
False

```

### Check if values are present within the data structure

```python
>>> ds = DisjointSet()
>>> "a" in ds
False

>>> ds.find("a")
'a'

>>> "a" in ds
True

```

### List elements and sets within the disjoint set

```python
>>> ds = DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2, 3: 3})
>>> list(ds)
[(1, 2), (2, 2), (3, 3)]

>>> ds = DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2, 3: 3})
>>> list(ds.itersets())
[{1, 2}, {3}]

```

## Contributing

Feel free to open any issues on github.

## Authors

- [Maciej Rapacz](https://github.com/mrapacz/)

classes.name: disjoint_set.utils.IdentityDict
documentation: A defaultdict implementation which places the requested key as its value in case it's missing.

classes.name: disjoint_set.main.InvalidInitialMappingError
documentation: Runtime error raised when invalid initial mapping causes the find() methods to change during iteration.

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__init__
documentation:
Disjoint set data structure.

The data structure can be initialized as an empty disjoint set:
\>>> DisjointSet()
DisjointSet({})

But it can also be instantiated from an existing mapping such as:
\>>> DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2})
DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2})

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.from_iterable
documentation: Instantiate a DistjointSet instance by transforming each element from an interable to a canonical element.

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__len__
documentation: Return the number of elements in the disjoint set.

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__contains__
documentation: Return True if `item` is an element of the disjoint set (not necessarily a canonical one).

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__bool__
documentation: Return True if disjoint set contains at least one element.

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__eq__
documentation:
Return True if both DistjoinSet structures are equivalent.

This may mean that their canonical elements are different, but the sets they form are the same.
\>>> DisjointSet({1: 1, 2: 1}) == DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2})
True

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__repr__
documentation:
Print self in a reproducible way.

```pycon
>>> DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2})
DisjointSet({1: 2, 2: 2})
```

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.__iter__
documentation: Iterate over items and their canonical elements.

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.itersets
documentation:
Yield sets of connected components.

If with_canonical_elements is set to True, method will yield tuples of (\<canonical_element>, \<set of elements>)
\>>> ds = DisjointSet()
\>>> ds.union(1,2)
\>>> list(ds.itersets())
\[{1, 2}\]
\>>> list(ds.itersets(with_canonical_elements=True))
\[(2, {1, 2})\]

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.find
documentation:
Return the canonical element of a given item.

In case the element was not present in the data structure, the canonical element is the item itself.
\>>> ds = DisjointSet()
\>>> ds.find(1)
1
\>>> ds.union(1, 2)
\>>> ds.find(1)
2

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.union
documentation:
Attach the roots of x and y trees together if they are not the same already.

```
param x

first element

param y

second element
```

methods.name: disjoint_set.main.DisjointSet.connected
documentation:
Return True if x and y belong to the same set (i.e. they have the canonical element).

```pycon
>>> ds = DisjointSet()
>>> ds.connected(1, 2)
False
>>> ds.union(1, 2)
>>> ds.connected(1, 2)
True
```
